rulings.Case Title: TERRA NOVA INDUSTRIES, A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION VS. M/M WINE REALTY, LLC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendants Brian Mcgonigle, Big Wines, Inc And Non-Party World Beverage Championship, Llc Notice Of Petition And Petition To Vacate Partial Final Arbitration Award
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday October 17, 2018 line 2. DEFENDANT BIG WINES, INC., BRIAN MCGONIGLE Defendants Brian Mcgonigle, Big Wines, Inc And Non-Party World Beverage Championship, Llc Notice Of Petition And Petition To Vacate Partial Final Arbitration Award DENIED. Moving party failed to show that the challenged findings of the arbitrator stem from errors of law of legal reasoning. Moving party also failed to cite any authority for the proposition that this Court may disturb arbitrator's factual findings. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: LISA J. BAILLY-SOLWAY ET AL VS. HILARY C. PIERCE ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday October 17, 2018 line 3. CROSS DEFENDANT SEAN SOLWAY, PACIFIC UNION INTERNATIONAL INC. DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT SUSTAINED without leave to amend. Cross-Complainant fails to cite any authority for the proposition that broker/agent should have read the contractual provision re: exclusive possession of the garage; realized that the circuits are in the garage; researched the law on access to circuits; advised Cross-Complainant that the contractual provision would constitute a violation of code. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: CONCHITA VILLAROMAN CLARK ET AL VS. RICHARD VILLAROMAN ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiffs/Cross-Defendants' Motion For A Protective Order
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday October 17, 2018 line 4. Plaintiffs/Cross-Defendants' Motion For A Protective Order Hearing Required to address partial transfer of personal property into Plaintiff's custody and control and whether Defendant is still willing to provide the keys and/or restore possession of the unit/real property to Plaintiff at this time. Parties are ordered to appear at 8:30 a.m. and engage in meaningful meet and confer efforts. Parties are to report of their progress at 9:30 a.m. Parties may be required to continue their meet and confer efforts thereafter. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: LAI FONG LEONG ET AL VS. CHAN 1985 TRUST ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Defendants Special Motion To Strike Anti Slapp
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday October 17, 2018 line 7. DEFENDANT CHAN 1985 TRUST, PAULINE CHAN, LAWRENCE CHAN Notice Of Defendants Special Motion To Strike Anti Slapp DENIED as to the first and third causes of action. Claims stated in these causes of action do not arise out of protected activity. They arise out of failure to accommodate and discriminatory conduct of Plaintiff. While these causes of action not not pleaded perfectly and some allegations appear to be misplaced (references to SFRO 37.9(a)(8) (FAC 13:1); damages caused by failure to make necessary repairs (FAC 11:3); non-tenant making claims under the Rent Ordinance (FAC 10:22-24; 13:16-18), the claims as alleged do not arise out of protected activity. = (501/REQ)
